results.possibleIdentity: Karel Novak was an electrical engineer active during the late 1960s and 1970s, based in New York City. He was employed by Syska and Hennessy (now Syska Hennessy Group), a prominent consulting engineering firm specializing in mechanical and electrical systems for high-rise buildings and major infrastructure projects. Novak was an associate or volunteer with Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), a non-profit organization co-founded by Billy Klüver and Robert Rauschenberg to promote collaborations between the arts and engineering. He was documented in the E.A.T. Technical Services registry, a database of engineers who offered their technical expertise to artists. His professional background in electrical engineering aligned with Syska and Hennessy's involvement in complex building systems in New York and internationally, including collaborations on major exhibitions and public works during that era.
